title: SendGrid Email Templates for Account Member Notifications
description:
Create SendGrid email templates and notification logic for account member lifecycle events:

1. **Enable member** - notification when an account member is enabled (previously only disable/revoke was implemented)
2. **Disable member** - already implemented with 'revoke' subject line
3. **Over seat limit** - admin notification when the team exceeds available user seats
4. **Upgrade/downgrade seats** - notification when the available seat count changes

Also implemented a private API endpoint to programmatically downgrade user seat counts, accepting a team ID and new user number. This endpoint updates available users and sends the downgrade notification email. This endpoint is classified as a private (internal-only) API, not exposed to customers.
acceptance_criteria: SendGrid template exists for member enable notification
SendGrid template exists for over-limit seat notification to admin
SendGrid template exists for seat upgrade/downgrade notification
Enable notification triggers when an account member is re-enabled
Over-limit notification triggers when team exceeds available seats
Private API endpoint accepts team_id and user_number for seat downgrade
Downgrade endpoint is not exposed in the public API
middleware_changes: Email notification service extended with new SendGrid templates for enable, over-limit, and upgrade/downgrade events. Notification triggers wired into the member lifecycle actions.
api_changes: Private endpoint POST /api/v1_0/removeUser accepts {team_id, user_number} to programmatically downgrade available seats. Requires Bearer token authentication. Not intended for public API exposure.
implementation_notes: The downgrade API endpoint was flagged as needing to be private (internal use only, accessible via Zapier or Salesforce automation). It should not be included in the public-facing API documentation. The endpoint uses Bearer token authentication via the /user/tokens route.
comment_summary: Developer implemented enable/disable templates and a downgrade API endpoint. Product owner clarified that the downgrade endpoint should be a private API (not customer-accessible), usable only via internal Zapier/Salesforce connections. This distinction between public and private APIs was an important architectural decision.
